summary: Training, inference, compute, and sandbox stack for company-owned AI agents.
editor_note: AI infrastructure is moving from renting prompts to owning feedback loops. Prime Intellect puts compute, training, inference, sandboxes, and Lab post-training behind a `pip install prime` entry point. Unlike model APIs alone, the bet is company-owned agent improvement. The risk is whether $130M Series A expectations turn into repeatable customer results.
why_featured: $130M Series A for open agent stack

product_evidence.claim: The official site describes a stack for training, deploying, and continuously improving AI agents across Training, Inference, Compute, and Research surfaces.
The official docs expose a product entry point for developers to install Prime and start working with the platform from a command line.
The Lab announcement describes task specification, evaluation harnesses, reward signals, rollouts, adapter training, and deployment for self-improving agents.

market_context.primary_user: AI platform teams, applied AI labs, and engineering teams building company-owned agents.
market_context.differentiation: Prime Intellect packages compute, RL/post-training, evals, sandboxes, and inference as one owned training loop rather than only hosted model access.
market_context.why_now: More teams are trying to turn production traces and task outcomes into model improvement loops instead of stopping at prompt orchestration.

risks.claim: The falsifiable risk is whether customer-owned agent training loops produce durable gains outside early design-partner and research-heavy deployments.
risks.basis: VentureDex assessment from the official stack, docs, Lab post, and Series A announcement.
